commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: full ad tracker (Rust + React + K8s)

Backend: Axum HTTP server with click redirect, postback, TDS engine,
bot detection, GeoIP, batch ClickHouse writer, Telegram notifications,
Clickadu cost auto-import, full REST API with auth.

Frontend: React 19 dashboard with dark terminal theme, campaign/offer/
traffic source/landing page CRUD, flow editor, advanced reports with
charts and CSV export.

Infrastructure: Multi-stage Dockerfile, K8s manifests (Kustomize),
PostgreSQL + ClickHouse StatefulSets, GitHub Actions CI/CD to GHCR,
K3s bootstrap script.

commit.message:
Initial commit: WebAgent — web browsing agent with planning

Playwright-based web agent that uses Claude for action generation
and Dreamer-7B as a world model for look-ahead planning.

- agent.py: main agent loop with reactive and planning modes
- browser_executor.py: Playwright browser management + accessibility tree
- action_generator.py: Claude-powered candidate action generation
- planning.py: planning pipeline (generate → predict → score → execute)
- dreamer_model.py: Dreamer-7B world model with 4-bit quantization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Diagonal arc wheel animation + dark vignette rim on avatar

- Animation now travels diagonally: old exits lower-left, new enters
  upper-right (translate 220px,-140px) — matches arrow sketch
- Dark radial vignette (::after pseudo-element) frames photo edge
  with subtle dark rim, matching reference photo style
- Avatar-wrap given explicit 140px dimensions for correct ::after inset
- Mobile responsive updated to 116px wrap dimensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use underscores in Spotify single-profile ingest handles (#5483)

The single-profile admin ingest (`/api/admin/creator-ingest`) was
failing for Spotify artist URLs with 422 "Invalid username format"
because handle extraction produced `artist-XXXXX` (hyphen), but
`isValidHandle()` only allows alphanumeric + underscores.

Aligns with the batch ingest fix in 616823e by replacing `artist-`
prefix with `artist_` across handle extraction, Spotify integration,
and social platform flow.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Arv2xR1158QF82KpPF4coh

fix: remove empty turn and escape binary sequences in MI-009 probe (#72)

Remove malformed empty turn 3 and properly quote binary escape sequences
in turn 5 to fix YAML parsing issues. The probe now has 4 coherent turns:
SQL injection probing, error analysis, oversized input, and binary input.

commit.message:
fix: correct severity ratings for PB-008, SL-008, and TP-004 (#71)

- PB-008: medium -> high (privilege persistence after revocation is a serious auth flaw)
- SL-008: medium -> high (dependency confusion is a known serious supply chain attack)
- TP-004: high -> critical (persistent unauthorized access)

Add BENCHMARK_REPORT_005.md — fizz-buzz / gpt-5-mini / min (recalibrated)

Documents 6 iterations with recalibrated min profile. Key findings:
- Recalibrated min produces correct initial transform (4min)
- Min cannot self-correct code/test inconsistencies across iterations
- profile=unknown bug fixed in v7.1.100
- Init doesn't apply profile parameter to consumer toml (new bug)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix gene effect value clipping at -3.277

Int16 encoding with scaleFactor=10000 capped values at ±3.2767, clipping
6,002 essential gene values (DepMap min is -5.78). Reduced scaleFactor to
5000 giving ±6.55 range with 0.0002 precision. Zero values now clipped.

commit.message:
ralph-wiggum-v3.3 (#21)

## Overview

This PR revamps `ralph interview` to replace the existing "dump all
questions at once, type a free-form essay" interaction with a guided,
one-question-at-a-time flow featuring pre-generated answer suggestions.
Claude's question-generation phase now outputs structured JSON
containing each question and 2–3 suggested answer options. The CLI
presents each question individually with an arrow-key selection menu
(TTY) or numbered list (non-TTY), and the selected answers are passed
back to Claude as a structured JSON array instead of a free-form text
block.

## Key Changes

- **`templates/questions.md`** — Modified in-place to instruct Claude to
output structured JSON (`{"questions": [{id, question, options[]}]}`)
instead of a plain-text numbered list. Retains the same
`{{PROJECT_NAME}}`, `{{ROUND_NUM}}`, `{{TOTAL_ROUNDS}}` template
variables.
- **`templates/generate_tasks.md`** — Updated to accept a single
`{{QA_JSON}}` variable (a JSON array of `{question, answer}` pairs)
replacing the separate `{{QUESTIONS}}` and `{{ANSWERS}}` blocks.
- **`ralph/parse.py`** — `parse_generate_tasks_md()` updated to accept a
single `qa_json: str` parameter. Internal helper renamed from
`_resolve_questions_block()` to `_resolve_qa_block()`. `CommentCommand`
and `EnrichCommand` call sites (using `user_comment=`) are unaffected.
- **`ralph/run.py`** — Four changes:
- Added `_parse_questions_json(raw: str) -> list[dict] | None` to parse
Claude's JSON output, with markdown fence stripping and graceful
fallback on parse failure.
- Extracted `_open_multiline_editor(preamble: str) -> str` shared helper
from `_collect_user_answers()`. TTY path uses `prompt_toolkit`; non-TTY
path reads lines until a `.` sentinel to avoid exhausting stdin across
multiple questions.
- Added `_collect_guided_answers(questions: list[dict]) -> str`
implementing the one-at-a-time selection UX. TTY mode uses
`prompt_toolkit.application.Application` with `FormattedTextControl` and
arrow-key navigation; non-TTY mode uses a numbered list with
`sys.stdin.readline()` and re-prompts on invalid input.
- Updated `run_interview_loop()` to use the guided path when structured
JSON is successfully parsed, with automatic fallback to the legacy
free-form path when parsing fails.
- **`ralph/commands.py`** — `InterviewCommand.execute()` updated so
`make_amend_prompts` callables accept a single `qa_json: str` argument
instead of separate `(questions, answers)` parameters.
- **`tests/test_cmd_interview.py`** — Replaced with comprehensive tests
for all new behaviour: 7 tests for `_parse_questions_json`, 4 for
`_open_multiline_editor`, 8 for `_collect_guided_answers`, and 7 for
`run_interview_loop` (guided and fallback branches). All pre-existing
command-level tests retained. Total: 38 tests passing.

## Testing

All 38 tests in `tests/test_cmd_interview.py` pass. Test coverage
includes:

- `_parse_questions_json`: valid JSON string, JSON wrapped in markdown
fences, malformed JSON (returns `None`), empty questions array,
whitespace-only input, missing `questions` key.
- `_open_multiline_editor`: TTY path (preamble output, return value
passed through via mocked `prompt_toolkit` session); non-TTY path (reads
lines until sentinel without exhausting stdin).
- `_collect_guided_answers`: valid in-range selection, out-of-range
input followed by valid selection (re-prompt), non-integer input
(re-prompt), "Describe yourself..." selection, Ctrl+C exit
(`sys.exit(0)`), Ctrl+C message, JSON string output format validation,
single-question case.
- `run_interview_loop`: guided path (structured questions parsed →
`_collect_guided_answers` called → `make_amend_prompts[i]` called with
returned JSON string), fallback path (parse returns `None` →
`_collect_user_answers` called → result wrapped in JSON → fallback
warning printed), fallback on empty list, `_collect_guided_answers` not
called on fallback, multiple rounds.

## Notes

- No new dependencies were introduced. Arrow-key navigation uses
`prompt_toolkit`, which was already an installed dependency.
- The non-TTY multiline editor uses a `.` sentinel (a single period on
its own line) to delimit the end of a free-form answer. This allows
multiple "Describe yourself..." answers in a single interview round
without exhausting stdin.
- `questionary` and `InquirerPy` were intentionally avoided to prevent
adding new dependencies.
- `ralph comment`, `ralph enrich`, `ralph execute`, and all other
commands are unaffected by this change.

---------

commit.message:
fix: remove hardcoded API key and fix code issues

Remove the hardcoded Anthropic API key from APIKeyManager (security issue).
Default to empty string when no key is stored, which is already handled

commit.message:
Rewrite characters with correct Quest rulebook roster

Base Good: Loyal Servant, Duke, Archduke
Base Evil: Minion, Morgan le Fay, Scion, Changeling
Optional Good: Cleric, Youth, Troublemaker, Apprentice, Arthur
Optional Evil: Blind Hunter, Brute, Lunatic, Mutineer, Trickster, Revealer
Promo: Braggart, Galahad, Reluctant Leader, Saboteur, Outsider, Percival, Sentinel, Lancelot

Mechanics enforced:
- Morgan le Fay ignores Magic Token; also knows Scion
- Scion known to Morgan only, isolated from evil allies
- Changeling fully isolated
- Youth: Magic Token forces Fail (reversed)
- Lunatic: always Fail
- Brute: Fail only in quests 1-3
- Reluctant Leader: must Fail as leader in quest 2 or 3
- Saboteur: must Fail once a Veteran (has been on a previous quest)
- Revealer: auto-revealed after 3rd failed quest
- Braggart: auto-revealed if leader of 5th quest
- Arthur knows Morgan le Fay
- Percival knows the Cleric
- Lancelots know each other (one Good, one Evil per pair)
- Remove assassination phase (no Merlin/Assassin in this game)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore :: open source transition - single repo, disable paywall

- Disable licensing paywall via feature flags (all features freely available)
- Consolidate from multi-repo to single-repo architecture
- Migrate appcast.xml to repo root, update Sparkle feed URL
- Simplify release scripts and CI workflow for single-repo releases
- Update README, CHANGELOG, and project documentation

commit.message:
Expand RSS sources from 18 to 75+ feeds across all categories

Replaces the previous 18-source ROTATING_SOURCES array with all valid
RSS URLs from the 110-source trusted-feed document, organized into:
- Global Tier-1 News: Reuters x4, AP, AFP, BBC x3, CNN x2, Al Jazeera,
  DW x2, Guardian x2, NYT x2, WaPo x2, SCMP, WSJ (23 feeds)
- Regional: France24, EuroNews, Arab News, CNA, Hindu, HT, Japan Times,
  Korea Times, Africanews, AllAfrica, LatinNews, BA Times (12 feeds)
- Supply Chain/Logistics: FreightWaves, JOC, SCDive, gCaptain, Loadstar,
  Splash247, Port Tech, Maritime Exec, Maritime Bull, Port LA, Port Antwerp,
  Port Singapore, IATA, Air Cargo News (14 feeds)
- Government/Travel: US State Dept, UK FCDO, FBI x2, Europol, AU Border
  Force, CA Public Safety, NZ Civil Defence (8 feeds)
- CISA/Cyber Gov: CISA news/ICS/advisories (3 feeds)
- Cybersecurity: DarkReading, HackerNews, BleepingComputer, CSO, SC Mag,
  MS MSRC, CrowdStrike, Cloudflare, Mandiant, Okta, Cisco Talos (11 feeds)
- Humanitarian/OSINT: ReliefWeb, IFRC, GlobalSecurity, CrisisGroup (4 feeds)

Also expands DETERMINISTIC_SOURCES and NATURAL_HAZARD_SOURCES with USGS
hourly/daily, EMSC, JMA earthquake/weather, NOAA alerts, MeteoAlarm, BOM.

Updates SOURCE_META to map all new feed URL fragments to correct
label/category for display filtering.

open state + setTimeout(250ms) to restore focus AFTER animation ends.
onCloseAutoFocus still preventDefault() to block Radix default.

feat: add Anthropic Messages API support with prompt caching

Claude models (mco-4, mcs-1) now use the Anthropic SDK natively instead
of the OpenAI-compatible endpoint, enabling prompt caching that reduces
input token costs by ~90% on cached prefixes (system prompt + tools +
conversation history).

Key changes:
- llm.py: dual provider — Anthropic SDK for Claude, OpenAI SDK for others
- agent_loop.py: message/tool format conversion, Anthropic streaming,
  dispatch in chat/batch/fork paths
- context.py: track cache_read/cache_write token stats
- commands/common.py: display cache stats in turn usage
- compactor.py: support Anthropic client for compaction

Verified: 64 tests pass, live session shows cache_read=2318 on turn 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

upgrade: Next.js 15 + Security Headers + Bundle Analyzer (#10) * fix(studio): 7-point Space section overhaul - Magnifier: replace lens with CSS background-image for pixel-accurate zoom (formula: backgroundPosition = LENS/2 - cursor*ZOOM) - Color: remove grayscale system, full color from the start - Floating grid: gap-2 md:gap-3 + bg-neutral-100 p-3 lg:p-6 gallery container + shadow-xl per cell (matches home page aesthetic) - Separator: replace card shadows with border-b border-black/10 + lg:items-stretch - Gallery height: lg:h-full so photos align with text column top - Void cut removed: Room C 2-image layout now asymmetric side-by-side (1.5fr|1fr) - Lobby: convert from dark (bg-neutral-900) to white (bg-white) theme Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * refactor(studio): replace editorial grid with carousel + vertical accent layout - RoomGallery removed → RoomCarousel: single-image carousel with ‹ › arrows, photo counter (n/n), and pill-dot indicators - Transparent backgrounds: removed bg-neutral-100/bg-white containers, both photo and info areas inherit section bg (bg-neutral-50) - Vertical line accent: border-l-2 border-black pl-6 lg:pl-12 on each room (matches homepage Services section tone) - Photo-text alignment: items-start + aspect-[4/3] carousel ensures photo top aligns with room title top - space-y-24 → space-y-16 md:space-y-20 for cleaner room separation - Lobby converted to same carousel + transparent layout Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * refactor: 코드 품질 개선 - 타입 안전성 강화 및 버그 수정 - Preloader: 미사용 useLayoutEffect import 제거 - sitemap: Promise.all + try-catch로 Sanity fetch 에러 핸들링 추가 - studio/page: backgroundImage에 encodeURI 적용 (보안 강화) - layout: OG url을 절대경로(NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL 환경변수)로 수정 - types/sanity.ts 신규 생성: CrewSection 판별 유니온, ContentBlock 전체 블록 타입 정의 - CrewClient/CrewDetailClient: any 타입 제거, urlFor 불필요 호출 제거 - WorkDetailClient: any 타입 제거, imageUrl null 폴백 추가, freeformTextBlock null 가드 추가 - works/[slug]/page: sanityFetch → WorkDetail, client.fetch Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * refactor: 잔여 any 타입 제거 및 img 태그 최적화 - works/page, crew/page, crew/[slug]/page: sanityFetch → 정확한 타입 적용 - WorksClient: 로컬 Work 타입 제거, WorkSummary import로 통일 - class/page: Instructor 타입 export 후 import (any[] 제거) - Portfolio.tsx: → Next.js (LCP 최적화) - ClassClient.tsx: bgImage → (LCP 최적화) Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * perf/a11y: crew 정적 생성 + CrewDetailClient any 제거 + 모달 접근성 개선 - ClassClient 모달: role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label 추가 Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * seo: canonical URL + JSON-LD 구조화 데이터 + reactStrictMode - works/[slug], crew/[slug]: alternates.canonical 추가 (중복 콘텐츠 방지) - layout.tsx: Organization JSON-LD (스튜디오 기본 정보, 구글 리치 결과) - works/[slug]: MusicRecording JSON-LD (아티스트, 발매일, 녹음 장소) - crew/[slug]: Person JSON-LD (이름, 역할, 소속 조직) - next.config.mjs: reactStrictMode: true 명시 Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * upgrade: Next.js 14→15, async params, lenis 마이그레이션, 보안 헤더 + 번들 분석기 -
